Company Description

Let’s create a more sociable future together

At Endeavour, we’re totally into what we do. With a portfolio that includes Dan Murphy’s, BWS, ALH Hotels, Pinnacle Drinks and more, we love to bring people together. Together we share our passion for our products and industry; it’s what inspires us to dream big, and continue to create new experiences for our customers and teams across Australia. If you thrive on positive energy, we want to meet you!

Job Description
Love what you do and leave your mark
Melbourne or Sydney location 
Own the Process. Drive the future.

We're looking for a highly capable Process Excellence Owner to take full accountability for the design, governance, and continuous improvement of one or more critical end-to-end enterprise processes.

You will hold the keys to our most critical end-to-end enterprise workflows. This is a high-impact role where you don't just follow a map you draw it. You will take accountability for the health, agility, and strategic direction of our core processes, ensuring they are not only compliant but are competitive advantages for the Group

You are a seasoned professional who understands that process is the heartbeat of a business. You have a proven track record of managing massive, multi-layered stakeholder environments and aren't afraid to challenge the status quo to ensure superior performance.

Sound good? Read on.

Here is a taster of what you can expect in this role:

Define the process vision, mission, objectives, goals, and associated KPIs and controls in alignment with the enterprise framework.
Hold direct accountability for the assigned end-to-end process, working with the relevant Business Owners, Data Owners, and Systems/Product Owners.
Lead and sponsor process improvement initiatives, including automation, simplification, and standardisation, to deliver efficiency and value.
Facilitate governance forums, monitor performance and compliance, and provide governance oversight for process changes driven by transformation initiatives.
Play a key role in rolling out and championing process governance and supporting frameworks across the groups and with your business partners 
Partner with business units and IT to drive alignment, accountability, and ownership of process activities, acting as the key escalation point for process-related issues, risks, or conflicts.
Oversee accurate and consistent process documentation within SAP Signavio (or similar platforms), ensuring adherence to enterprise standards.
Qualifications

Now let’s talk about you: 

You are a master of stakeholder diplomacy who can navigate complex matrixed environments, manage external service partners, and implement rigorous compliance controls that align with global regulatory standards.

A proven track record (typically 7-10+ years) of leading and transforming complex, global enterprise processes (e.g., Lead-to-Cash, Source-to-Pay, or Record-to-Report).
Bachelor’s degree in Business, IT, Engineering, or a related discipline.
Proven experience in business process management and transformation having completed similar roles as a process or global process owner 
The ability to navigate and influence a matrixed organization. You are comfortable acting as the single point of accountability and can manage expectations between internal business units and external service providers.
Certification in Lean Six Sigma or an equivalent process improvement methodology.
Proven expertise in SAP Signavio or similar process management platforms.
Demonstrated capability with industry-standard process modelling frameworks (BPMN 2.0, BPML, EPC, APQC PCF, etc.).
Experience working effectively within an outsourced service model environment.
Experience in Retail, FMCG, and Hospitality industries (desirable)
Experience in large-scale corporate environments, particularly with IT-enabled transformations (desirable)
Exposure to formal governance, compliance, and risk management frameworks (desirable)

​​​​​​The benefits are good too! 

We offer hybrid working 
An exclusive discount for BWS, Dan Murphy’s, Woolworths, BIG W and other Endeavour Group brands, including our ALH pubs
Your health and wellbeing is your most important asset, and as one of our valued team members, it’s our first priority. You will have a range of free services to help you live well and support your physical, mental and financial wellbeing
Endeavour Group is full of opportunities - use our dedicated learning and development options to grow an idea, yourself, and your career. This is just the start, so dream big.

At Endeavour, we value being a workplace where everyone’s welcome - if you meet a number of the requirements (and not all), we encourage you to apply.

Additional Information

We are together creators

With a portfolio that includes Dan Murphy’s, BWS, ALH Hotels, Pinnacle Drinks and more, Endeavour Group is big on sociability. Together we create the moments that bring millions of people together. And together we have more fun, create more opportunities, and score a lot more goals. We’re serious about creating a safe, inclusive and fun place to rock up to where equal opportunity is key, and flexibility is part of how we roll.  

We’re all about creating a more sociable future - for our customers and each other. If this job excites you - and you’re close-enough on the requirements, reach out, we’d love to hear from you.

You can learn more about working with us on LinkedIn or at endeavourgroupcareers.com.au.

Our Talent Team and Hiring Leaders kindly request no unsolicited resumes or approaches from Recruitment Agencies. Endeavour Group is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

The Company

Our client is a market-leading ASX-listed property investment group with a high-profile leadership team, strong access to institutional capital and a clearly defined growth strategy. The business is highly acquisitive, continues to launch new funds and manages a large and diversified portfolio spanning premium Commercial, Industrial and Retail assets.

The Role

The Group FP&A Manager sits at the centre of financial planning, analysis and strategic decision-making for the organisation. Working closely with senior leadership and teams across the business, the role supports the delivery of group forecasting, budgeting, performance analysis and capital planning.

Key Responsibilities
Partner with divisional teams to understand key drivers of performance, risks and opportunities, and provide commercial insights.
Maintain and enhance group forecasting models across the income statement, balance sheet and cash flow.
Coordinate and deliver the group budgeting and long-term forecasting process, including consolidation of divisional inputs.
Support capital management through analysis of funding requirements, debt metrics and financial data for rating agencies.
Contribute to monthly and quarterly reporting for senior leadership, including analysis and commentary on group performance.
Assist with preparation of external reporting materials including results presentations, investor updates and other market communications.
Identify and implement improvements to financial planning tools, processes and reporting frameworks.
Skills & Experience
CA/CPA/CFA qualified.
5+ years’ experience in FP&A, financial planning, corporate finance or a similar analytical role.
Strong financial modelling, analytical and problem-solving skills.
Advanced Excel capability and strong presentation skills in PowerPoint and Word.
Experience working with large data sets and business intelligence tools such as Power BI is desirable.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills with the ability to translate financial insights for senior audiences.

Senior Data Engineer / Snowflake / Sydney / Permanent / $160,000 + super

About the company

A growing technology consultancy based in Sydney, focused on Data, Cloud, and AI solutions. This business supports clients with scalable, innovative technologies and offers a collaborative, fast-paced environment with space for growth and experimentation.

About the role

This Senior Data Engineer role sits within a small, high-performing team. You’ll take the lead on delivering Snowflake-centric data engineering solutions—from solution design and architecture through to implementation and optimisation. You’ll mentor junior engineers, guide best practices, and work across modern cloud platforms that heavily leverage Snowflake as the core data environment.

Benefits
$160,000 + Super
Work with cutting-edge Snowflake, cloud, and AI technologies
Supportive and innovative team culture
Flexible working arrangements
Opportunities to grow and take ownership of solutions
Duties/Key responsibilities
Design, build, and optimise robust Snowflake data pipelines, models, and warehouse solutions
Lead client delivery engagements and manage key stakeholder relationships
Mentor junior team members and champion Snowflake and cloud engineering best practices
Work across cloud-based platforms including AWS / GCP and Snowflake, with exposure to Databricks where relevant
Skills and experience
Strong experience in Snowflake architecture, data modelling, ETL/ELT processes, and scalable data pipelines
Proficient in Python, SQL, and/or Spark
Hands-on experience with Snowflake and complementary cloud environments such as Google Cloud or Databricks
Comfortable working directly with clients in consultative, solution-focused engagements
Knowledge of visualisation tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) and DevOps practices
Bonus: experience with Snowpipe, streaming data, big data frameworks, or data governance and security best practices
If you’re interested in this opportunity and feel you’re the right fit please click “apply”

About Allura Partners
Allura Partners was founded to support private equity firms by placing C-Suites to lead, transform, and scale portfolio companies. Today, in addition to our proven private equity specialisation, we’ve become a trusted partner for major ASX-listed and private enterprises in Australia, offering executive search, recruitment, and business transformation. Known for integrity, reliability, and results, our expertise spans Executive Search, Accounting & Finance, Transformation & Change, and Technology & Digital. We connect businesses and top-tier talent, exceeding expectations and driving lasting impact.
